top_findings.summary: The skill, primarily described for encrypting and decrypting data or validating encryption implementations, requests broad permissions including Bash commands for security scanning and auditing (e.g., Bash(scan:*), Bash(audit:*)) and access to the entire codebase and configuration files. These permissions are excessive for basic encryption/decryption tasks, as they allow actions unrelated to the core purpose, such as system-wide vulnerability scans or compliance audits, which could intrude on unrelated files and processes without clear necessity for simple data protection operations.
